message:
feat(fwc): add doctor diagnosis engine, rate limit dashboard, fix pipeline wiring

- doctor.rs (CUAL-J.2): Auto-diagnosis for auth/rate-limit/network/latency/TLS/config
  issues with fix-it recipe suggestions. 45 tests.
- rate_limit.rs (CUAL-I.1): Rate limit tracking with pool snapshots, usage classification
  (OK/WARNING/CRITICAL), connector aggregation, budget estimation. 38 tests.
- Wire pipeline_dispatch and events_dispatch (from other agents' code).
- Fix pipe.rs raw string delimiter for #general.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.5","title":"[CUAL-A.5] MCP resource and prompt exposure for connector state and docs","description":"## Goal
Expose FCP connector state and documentation as MCP resources and prompts, enabling agents to inspect connector health, rate limits, schemas, and usage patterns without invoking operations.

## MCP Resources
- resource://connector/{id}/health         -> HealthSnapshot
- resource://connector/{id}/rate-limits    -> RateLimitStatus per pool
- resource://connector/{id}/operations     -> Vec<OperationInfo> summary
- resource://connector/{id}/history        -> Recent operation audit log
- resource://connectors/status             -> Cross-connector health dashboard

## MCP Prompts
- prompt://connector/{id}/how-to-use       -> Usage guide with examples
- prompt://connector/{id}/op/{op}/example  -> Specific operation example
- prompt://connector/{id}/troubleshoot     -> Troubleshooting guide

## Why Resources Matter for Agents
Agents need context before deciding which tool to call. MCP resources let them inspect state cheaply (no tool invocation side effects). Prompts provide structured guidance that helps agents use tools correctly.

## Deliverables
- Resource handlers for health, rate-limits, operations, history
- Prompt handlers for usage guides and examples
- Tests with mock MCP client reading resources and prompts","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.958602Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.958602Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","mcp","prompts","resources"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.958602Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.10.1","title":"[CUAL-J.1] Cross-connector health aggregation dashboard","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc health' that shows health status of all configured connectors at a glance, with drill-down for unhealthy ones.

## Commands
- fwc health                               # All connectors
- fwc health github                        # One connector detail
- fwc health --unhealthy                   # Only show degraded/error
- fwc health --json                        # Machine-readable
- fwc health --watch 30s                   # Refresh every 30s

## Output
Connector   Status    Auth     Latency  Last Check  Issues
github      healthy   ok       45ms     2m ago      -
slack       healthy   ok       120ms    1m ago      -
twilio      degraded  ok       890ms    30s ago     High latency
jira        error     EXPIRED  -        5m ago      Token expired

## How It Works
1. For each configured connector, call handle_health()
2. Also check auth status from credential store (Track C)
3. Include latest latency from metrics
4. Highlight issues: auth expired, high latency, error state

## Deliverables
- Health aggregator calling handle_health() across connectors
- Auth status integration from credential store
- fwc health command with --unhealthy filter
- Watch mode for continuous monitoring
- Tests: healthy, degraded, error, auth-expired scenarios","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.251009Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.251009Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","dashboard","health","monitoring"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.10.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.251009Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.10.2","title":"[CUAL-J.2] Auto-diagnosis with fix-it recipes for common issues","description":"## Goal
When a connector is unhealthy, automatically diagnose the issue and suggest specific fix-it commands.

## Commands
- fwc doctor github                        # Diagnose GitHub connector
- fwc doctor --all                         # Diagnose all connectors
- fwc doctor --fix github                  # Apply automatic fixes where safe

## Diagnosis Rules
1. Auth failure (401/403) -> 'fwc auth test github' then 'fwc auth add github --token ...'
2. Rate limited (429) -> 'Wait 12 minutes for reset' or 'Reduce batch concurrency'
3. High latency -> 'Check network' or 'Try different API endpoint'
4. Connection refused -> 'Service may be down, check status page: https://...'
5. Config invalid -> 'fwc config doctor github' to validate and repair
6. Certificate error -> 'Check system time' or 'Update CA certificates'

## Auto-Fix
Safe auto-fixes (--fix mode):
- Refresh expired OAuth tokens
- Clear stale caches
- Reset circuit breakers
- Retry failed health checks

Unsafe fixes (require confirmation):
- Re-authenticate
- Change configuration
- Restart connector

## Deliverables
- Diagnosis rule engine (pattern -> suggestion mapping)
- fwc doctor command
- Auto-fix executor for safe fixes
- Confirmation flow for unsafe fixes
- Tests: each diagnosis rule, auto-fix scenarios","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.384507Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.384507Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","diagnosis","fix-it","health"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.10.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.384507Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.11.1","title":"[CUAL-K.1] Deep schema navigator with required/optional/example annotations","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc schema' that provides a rich, interactive-style schema exploration experience showing required vs optional fields, types, constraints, and examples at each level.

## Commands
- fwc schema github.create_issue                     # Full schema with annotations
- fwc schema github.create_issue --required-only     # Only required fields
- fwc schema github.create_issue --field labels       # Drill into one field
- fwc schema github.create_issue --examples          # Show example values
- fwc schema github.create_issue --output            # Show output schema

## Output (TOON default)
github.create_issue input schema:
  owner*     string   Repository owner (e.g. 'octocat')
  repo*      string   Repository name (e.g. 'hello-world')
  title*     string   Issue title (e.g. 'Bug report: login fails')
  body       string   Issue body in markdown
  assignees  [string] List of GitHub usernames to assign
  labels     [string] List of label names (e.g. ['bug', 'priority:high'])
  milestone  integer  Milestone number

* = required

## Why Agents Need This
Agents looking at raw JSON Schema see a wall of type definitions. This command presents schemas in a flat, scannable format with real examples pulled from ai_hints.examples. The --required-only flag is particularly valuable: agents can construct minimal valid inputs quickly.

## Deliverables
- JSON Schema walker/flattener
- Required vs optional annotation
- Example value extraction from ai_hints
- Nested field drill-down
- TOON and JSON output modes
- Tests: simple schema, nested objects, arrays, enums, all-required, all-optional","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.557977Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:12:48.315403Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T13:12:48.315385Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","exploration","navigator","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.11.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.557977Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1210,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.11.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Deep schema navigator (schema_nav.rs) with walk_schema, scaffold_template, filter_by_field. 45 tests. Wired into schema_dispatch with --required-only/--field/--examples/--scaffold flags. Also landed validate.rs (19 tests) for pre-invoke input validation. Commit 72503022.","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:12:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.11.2","title":"[CUAL-K.2] Input payload template generator with scaffolded JSON","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc template' that generates a fill-in-the-blanks JSON template for any operation, with placeholder values and documentation comments.

## Commands
- fwc template github.create_issue                   # Full template with all fields
- fwc template github.create_issue --required-only   # Only required fields
- fwc template github.create_issue --fill owner=octocat,repo=hello-world  # Pre-fill some values

## Output
{
  "owner": "<string:required>",
  "repo": "<string:required>",
  "title": "<string:required>",
  "body": "<string:optional>",
  "assignees": ["<string:optional>"],
  "labels": ["<string:optional>"],
  "milestone": "<integer:optional>"
}

## With --fill:
{
  "owner": "octocat",
  "repo": "hello-world",
  "title": "<string:required>",
  "body": "<string:optional>"
}

## Why Agents Need This
Instead of reading a schema and constructing JSON from scratch, agents can: (1) generate template, (2) fill in values, (3) invoke. This reduces errors from schema misinterpretation.

## Deliverables
- Template generator from JSON Schema
- Placeholder format with type and required/optional annotation
- --fill flag for partial pre-population
- --required-only for minimal templates
- Tests: various schema shapes, nested objects, arrays, pre-fill","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.712404Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:06:23.931827Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T13:06:23.931802Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","scaffold","schema","template"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.11.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.712404Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1208,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.11.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CUAL-K.2 complete: 'fwc template' (alias: scaffold) generates fill-in-the-blanks JSON from operation input schemas. Features: --required-only for minimal templates, --fill key=value,... for pre-populated values, handles nested objects, arrays, enums, defaults, examples. 16 unit tests (template.rs), 785 total fwc tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:06:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.2.2","title":"[CUAL-B.2] Context-aware operation suggestions and what-can-I-do queries","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc what-can-i-do' (or 'fwc suggest') that shows relevant operations based on current context: configured connectors, recent operations, zone, and stated goal.

## Commands
- fwc suggest                              # What can I do with my configured connectors?
- fwc suggest --goal 'manage my tasks'     # Goal-directed suggestions
- fwc suggest --after github.list_issues   # What commonly follows this operation?
- fwc suggest --connector slack            # What can I do with Slack specifically?
- fwc suggest --unused                     # Operations I haven't used yet

## How It Works
1. Survey all configured and healthy connectors
2. For goal-directed: use search engine (B.1) to find matching operations
3. For --after: use ai_hints.related fields to suggest next steps
4. For general: show categorized overview grouped by action family (read, write, manage, monitor)
5. Rank by: relevance to goal, safety (safe first), frequency of use, connector health

## Why Agents Need This
Agents often know WHAT they want to accomplish but not WHICH connector or operation to use. This is the 'exploration' mode that turns fwc from a tool-you-must-already-know into a tool-that-teaches-you.

## Deliverables
- Suggestion engine using search index + operation graph (related operations)
- fwc suggest command with all flags
- Grouped output by action family
- Tests for goal-directed, follow-up, and general suggestions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:55.542617Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:01:59.058091Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T13:01:59.058074Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["context","cual","discovery","suggestions"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.2.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:55.542617Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1200,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.2.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CUAL-B.2 complete: 'fwc suggest' (alias: what-can-i-do) with 3 modes: (1) --goal for goal-directed search via B.1 search engine, (2) --after for related operation follow-ups using ai_hints, (3) default overview grouped by action family (read/write/delete/manage/monitor). Filters: --connector, --risk, --grouped, --limit. 769 total fwc tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:01:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.2.3","title":"[CUAL-B.3] Smart connector auto-routing for ambiguous intents","description":"## Goal
When multiple connectors can fulfill an intent, automatically rank and suggest the best option based on context: connector health, zone authorization, rate limit headroom, recent success rate, and user preference history.

## Background
Many operations exist across multiple connectors: 'send a message' works via Slack, Discord, Telegram, etc. 'Create an issue' works via GitHub, Jira, Linear, etc. When an agent searches or invokes by intent, it needs intelligent routing.

## Routing Signals (Priority Order)
1. Zone authorization (must be allowed)
2. Connector health (must be healthy or degraded, not error)
3. Rate limit headroom (prefer connectors with more quota remaining)
4. Historical success rate (prefer connectors that succeed more often)
5. User/agent preference history (learned from past choices)
6. Safety tier (prefer lower risk for equivalent operations)
7. Latency (prefer faster connectors)

## Integration Points
- Used by fwc search to annotate results with 'recommended' flag
- Used by intent compiler (39v14) to select default connector
- Used by pipeline engine (Track F) to choose connectors in recipes

## Deliverables
- Routing score calculator with configurable signal weights
- Preference history store (simple file-based)
- Integration with search and intent compiler
- Tests: multi-connector scenarios, degraded routing, preference learning","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:55.895679Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:55.895679Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","discovery","intent","routing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:55.895679Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.3.1","title":"[CUAL-C.1] Keychain-backed credential store with CRUD commands","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc auth' command family for managing connector credentials: add, list, remove, and update credentials stored securely in the OS keychain.

## Commands
- fwc auth add github --token ghp_xxxx              # Store a token
- fwc auth add twilio --account-sid AC... --auth-token xx  # Multi-field auth
- fwc auth add slack --credential-id cred_xxx        # Secretless reference
- fwc auth list                                       # Show all stored credentials (redacted)
- fwc auth list --json                                # Machine-readable
- fwc auth remove github                              # Remove stored credential
- fwc auth show github                                # Show credential details (redacted)

## Storage Backend
1. Primary: OS keychain (macOS Keychain, Linux secret-service, Windows Credential Manager) via keyring crate
2. Fallback: encrypted file in ~/.fwc/credentials.enc (ChaCha20-Poly1305, key derived from machine identity)
3. Never: plaintext files, environment variables as primary store

## Security Invariants
- Credentials never appear in process arguments (use stdin or file for sensitive values)
- 'fwc auth list' always redacts actual secret values
- Credentials never appear in logs, traces, or error messages
- 'fwc auth show' displays metadata (connector, auth type, created_at, last_used) but NOT the secret

## Integration
- fwc invoke automatically looks up credentials from store (no --token flag needed)
- fwc auth add auto-detects auth type from connector manifest
- Supports both direct credentials and CredentialId references for secretless mode

## Deliverables
- crates/fwc/src/auth/store.rs with keychain and file backends
- fwc auth subcommand (add, list, show, remove, update)
- Redaction middleware for all credential display
- Tests: add/list/remove lifecycle, redaction verification, fallback to file backend","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:26:35.839437Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:16:30.483849Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T14:16:30.483834Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","credentials","cual","keychain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.3.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:26:35.839437Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1192,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: Use the 'keyring' crate (v3+) for cross-platform keychain access. Service name: 'fwc-credentials'. Entry key: connector ID (e.g., 'fcp.github'). Value: JSON-serialized credential struct. For the encrypted file fallback, use ChaCha20-Poly1305 (already in workspace dependencies) with a key derived from machine ID. CRITICAL: The credential store must integrate with the FWC config store (1g7z0.5) but keep secrets separate — config store holds non-secret configuration, credential store holds secrets. They reference each other by connector ID.","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:34:20Z"},{"id":1224,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CUAL-C.1 implemented: credential_store.rs with encrypted file backend (ChaCha20-Poly1305), auth method auto-detection (bearer, basic, OAuth2, API key, session, secretless, custom), CRUD operations, redaction middleware, CLI parsing helpers, connector ID validation. 47 tests. All 1173 fwc tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T14:15:30Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.3.2","title":"[CUAL-C.2] Auth test, verify, and health commands per connector","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc auth test' that verifies stored credentials work by performing a lightweight probe against each connector's API.

## Commands
- fwc auth test github                # Test GitHub credentials
- fwc auth test --all                 # Test all stored credentials
- fwc auth test --all --json          # Machine-readable results

## How It Works
1. Look up stored credential for the connector
2. Call connector's handle_health() which performs a lightweight API call (e.g., GET /user for GitHub)
3. Report: auth_valid (bool), connector_healthy (bool), latency_ms, expiry_info (if token has expiry)
4. For OAuth connectors: check token refresh eligibility

## Output
TOON default:
  github     ok   12ms  token valid, no expiry
  slack      ok   45ms  token valid, expires in 3d
  twilio     FAIL 0ms   401 Unauthorized - check auth_token

## Why Agents Need This
Before starting a multi-step workflow, agents should verify all required connectors are authenticated. One failed auth mid-pipeline wastes all prior work. 'fwc auth test --all' is the preflight check.

## Deliverables
- fwc auth test command
- Per-connector health probe via handle_health()
- Token expiry detection where available
- Structured error output with fix suggestions","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:26:36.107821Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:16:24.858895Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","cual","health","verify"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.3.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:26:36.107821Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.3.4","title":"[CUAL-C.4] Secretless credential injection end-to-end plumbing","description":"## Goal
Complete the end-to-end secretless credential injection flow: agents configure connectors with a CredentialId instead of raw secrets, and the egress proxy injects actual credentials at the network boundary.

## Background
FCP spec describes secretless connectors where credentials never enter the connector process. Instead:
1. Agent stores a CredentialId reference: fwc auth add github --credential-id cred_github_work
2. Connector sends requests with X-FCP-Credential-ID header instead of Authorization
3. Egress proxy (fcp-host) intercepts, looks up the real credential, injects it into the outgoing request
4. Connector never sees the raw secret

## Current State
- Connector code already supports CredentialId auth mode (GitHubAuth::CredentialId, TwilioAuth::CredentialId, etc.)
- Missing: egress proxy implementation, credential store on the proxy side, fwc integration

## Deliverables
- Credential store for the egress proxy (host-side)
- X-FCP-Credential-ID injection middleware in fcp-host
- fwc auth add --credential-id flow
- fwc auth map: link a CredentialId to an actual secret (admin-only, proxy-side)
- E2E test: agent configures with CredentialId, invoke succeeds via proxy injection","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:26:36.587901Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:26:36.587901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","cual","proxy","secretless"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.3.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:26:36.587901Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4.1","title":"[CUAL-D.1] Append-only operation audit log with structured history","description":"## Goal
Record every fwc operation invocation in a structured, append-only local audit log that agents can query for history, debugging, and replay.

## Log Entry Structure
{
  entry_id: uuid,
  timestamp: ISO8601,
  connector_id: 'fcp.github',
  operation_id: 'github.create_issue',
  zone: 'z:work',
  input_hash: blake3(input),    // NOT the full input (may contain secrets)
  input_summary: 'owner=octocat repo=hello-world title=Bug report',
  output_hash: blake3(output),
  output_summary: 'issue #43 created',
  status: 'success' | 'error' | 'timeout' | 'rate_limited' | 'denied',
  latency_ms: 234,
  error_code: null | 'FCP_ERR_RATE_LIMITED',
  idempotency_key: null | 'idem-xxx',
  agent_session: null | 'SunnyMoose-session-42',
  capability_token_hash: blake3(token),
}

## Commands
- fwc history                             # Show recent operations (last 20)
- fwc history --connector github          # Filter by connector
- fwc history --status error              # Filter by status
- fwc history --since 1h                  # Time range
- fwc history --json                      # Machine-readable
- fwc history <entry_id>                  # Show full details of one entry

## Storage
- Append-only JSONL file at ~/.fwc/history.jsonl
- Automatic rotation at 10MB (history.jsonl.1, .2, etc.)
- Never store raw input/output (only hashes and summaries) for security

## Why Agents Need This
- Debug failed operations: what exactly was sent and when?
- Avoid duplicate work: did I already create this issue?
- Replay: re-execute a successful operation with same/modified inputs
- Accountability: which agent did what and when?

## Deliverables
- History logger middleware that wraps every invoke/simulate call
- History query engine with filters
- fwc history command
- Rotation and cleanup logic
- Tests: log, query, rotate, filter","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:16.829979Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:23:25.311917Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T13:23:25.311898Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","cual","history","log"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:16.829979Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1212,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Append-only JSONL audit log (history.rs): HistoryEntry, HistoryStore, content_hash, summarize_input, filtered queries, rotation. 55 tests.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4.3","title":"[CUAL-D.3] Idempotent undo for reversible operations","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc undo' that reverses a previous operation where the operation type supports it (e.g., create -> delete, enable -> disable).

## Background
Some operations have natural inverses:
- github.create_issue -> github.close_issue (or delete)
- slack.send_message -> slack.delete_message
- todoist.create_task -> todoist.delete_task
- kubernetes.create_pod -> kubernetes.delete_pod
- connector.enable -> connector.disable

## How It Works
1. Look up operation in history
2. Check if operation has a declared inverse in manifest (new manifest field: inverse_operation)
3. If inverse exists: construct inverse input from original output (e.g., use created issue ID)
4. If no declared inverse: suggest manual reversal steps
5. Show preview and require confirmation
6. Execute inverse operation
7. Log undo in history with reference to original

## Manifest Extension
[provides.operations.'github.create_issue']
inverse_operation = 'github.delete_issue'
inverse_input_mapping = { 'owner' = '$.input.owner', 'repo' = '$.input.repo', 'issue_number' = '$.output.issue.number' }

## Deliverables
- Inverse operation lookup from manifest
- Input mapping from original output to inverse input
- fwc undo command
- Manual reversal guidance for non-reversible operations
- Tests: create-undo cycle, non-reversible handling","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:18.055862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:18.055862Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","history","reversible","undo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:18.055862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- fwc events tail slack --type message.new          # By event type

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8.3","title":"[CUAL-H.3] Event replay from checkpoint or sequence number","description":"## Goal
Add event replay capability: resume event consumption from a saved checkpoint or specific sequence number.

## Commands
- fwc events tail slack --checkpoint slack-cp.json   # Resume from checkpoint
- fwc events tail slack --from-seq 12345             # Resume from sequence
- fwc events replay slack --from 2026-03-09T14:00 --to 2026-03-09T15:00  # Time range

## Checkpoint Format
{"connector":"slack","last_seq":12345,"last_timestamp":"...","last_event_id":"..."}

## How It Works
1. On each event, update checkpoint file (atomic write)
2. On startup with --checkpoint, read last position and request replay from there
3. Connector subscribe() accepts optional start_from parameter
4. If connector does not support replay, emit warning and start from current

## Deliverables
- Checkpoint file writer (atomic, with temp file + rename)
- Checkpoint reader and subscribe-from-position logic
- Time-range replay for connectors that support it
- Fallback behavior for connectors without replay
- Tests: checkpoint save/load, replay, fallback","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:30:02.978451Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:30:02.978451Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","events","replay","streaming"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:30:02.978451Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8.4","title":"[CUAL-H.4] Event-triggered operation execution (reactive rules)","description":"## Goal
Add reactive rules that automatically invoke operations when matching events arrive, enabling event-driven automation.

## Rule Definition (TOML)
[[rules]]
name = 'bug-to-issue'
description = 'Create GitHub issue when Slack message mentions bug'
trigger = { connector = 'slack', type = 'message.new', match = 'data.text~bug|Bug|BUG' }
action = { connector = 'github', operation = 'create_issue', input = { owner = 'octocat', repo = 'hello-world', title = 'Bug report from Slack: {{event.data.text | truncate(80)}}', body = 'Reported by {{event.data.user}} in #{{event.data.channel}}\n\n{{event.data.text}}' } }
throttle = { max = 5, window = '1h' }  # Max 5 triggers per hour

## Commands
- fwc rules add my-rule.toml              # Register a rule
- fwc rules list                           # Show active rules
- fwc rules test my-rule.toml --event '...'  # Test with mock event
- fwc rules enable/disable rule-name       # Toggle rules
- fwc rules run                            # Start reactive event loop

## Safety
- Rules that invoke risky/dangerous operations require explicit approval at registration
- Throttle is mandatory to prevent runaway automation
- Dry-run mode: log what would happen without executing
- Circuit breaker: if rule triggers too many errors, auto-disable

## Deliverables
- Rule definition parser (TOML)
- Event matcher integrated with event filters (H.2)
- Action executor with template rendering
- Throttle enforcement
- Circuit breaker logic
- fwc rules command family
- Tests: match, throttle, dry-run, circuit breaker","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:30:03.158630Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:30:03.158630Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","events","reactive","streaming","triggers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:30:03.158630Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.9.2","title":"[CUAL-I.2] Automatic throttling and quota-aware scheduling","description":"## Goal
Integrate rate limit awareness into all execution paths (invoke, map, batch, pipeline) so operations are automatically throttled to stay within limits.

## Behavior
- Before each operation: check remaining quota in relevant rate limit pool
- If quota sufficient: proceed immediately
- If quota low (< 10%): slow down (add delay between operations)
- If quota exhausted: wait for reset, show countdown
- If multiple pools apply: respect the most restrictive

## Integration Points
- fwc invoke: single operation, simple check
- fwc map: distribute operations across time window to stay within limits
- fwc batch: schedule operations based on combined rate limit budget
- fwc pipeline: estimate total rate limit cost before starting

## User Control
- --no-throttle: bypass auto-throttling (agent accepts rate limit errors)
- --max-wait 30s: maximum time to wait for quota reset before giving up
- --rate-limit-strategy aggressive|conservative|balanced: tuning

## Deliverables
- Throttle middleware for all execution paths
- Quota prediction and scheduling algorithm
- Wait-for-reset with countdown display
- Integration with map, batch, pipeline executors
- Tests: throttle activation, wait-for-reset, strategy modes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:11.980898Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:11.980898Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","ratelimit","scheduling","throttle"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:11.980898Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- fwc rate-limits plan --batch ops.jsonl   # Rate limit feasibility for batch

Reset: in 12 minutes (full 5000 restored)
Recommendation: safe to proceed with 100 operations

## Deliverables
- Usage rate calculator from history
- Exhaustion time predictor
- Batch feasibility checker
- Optimal scheduling suggestions
- Tests: various usage patterns, forecast accuracy","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.115945Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.115945Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["budget","cual","forecast","ratelimit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:31:12.115945Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qtu","title":"fcp.grafana: Open-Source Observability Connector","description":"## Overview
Comprehensive observability connector for Grafana ecosystem, enabling AI agents to query metrics, analyze logs, inspect traces, and manage dashboards.

## Core Capabilities

### Metrics Querying (PromQL)
- Execute PromQL queries against Prometheus datasources
- Time range selection with relative and absolute times
- Aggregation and transformation functions
- Multi-datasource queries
- Query result formatting (tables, time series)

### Dashboard Access and Rendering
- List available dashboards by folder/tag
- Retrieve dashboard JSON definitions
- Render dashboard panels as images
- Variable interpolation for dynamic dashboards
- Export dashboards for backup/migration

### Alert Management
- List active and silenced alerts
- Query alert history and state transitions
- Create and modify alert rules
- Manage silence rules
- Acknowledge and annotate alerts

### Log Search (Loki Integration)
- LogQL query execution
- Log stream discovery
- Label-based filtering
- Pattern matching and parsing
- Log context retrieval around events

### Trace Inspection (Tempo Integration)
- Trace ID lookup and visualization
- Service map generation
- Span analysis and timing breakdown
- Cross-service correlation
- Trace-to-log linking

## Deployment Support

### Self-Hosted
- Direct API authentication
- Custom SSL/TLS configuration
- On-premise datasource access
- Air-gapped environment support

### Grafana Cloud
- Cloud API token authentication
- Stack management
- Usage metrics and billing info
- Multi-tenant organization support

## Implementation Notes
- Use Grafana HTTP API
- Support API key and OAuth authentication
- Handle rate limiting gracefully
- Cache frequently accessed dashboards

## Testing Requirements
- Mock Grafana server for unit tests
- Integration tests with test instance
- Performance tests for large queries
- Compatibility tests across Grafana versions","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:49.552569516Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T07:31:19.258883993Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T07:31:19.258883993Z","close_reason":"Duplicate/overlaps with flywheel_connectors-dcgw","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
